title: Section 130
content: If a person who is a party to a proceeding under this Act (not being a proceeding before 1*** or a court) dies pending the proceeding, the Registrar may, on request, and on proof to his satisfaction of the transmission of the interest of the deceased person, substitute in the proceeding his successor in interest in his place, or, if the Registrar is of opinion that the interest of the deceased person is sufficiently represented by the surviving parties, permit the proceeding to continue without the substitution of his successor in interest.
footnote: 1. The words “the Appellate Board or” omitted by Act 33 of 2021, s. 21 (w.e.f. 4-4-2021).
